summary: Tested QR PDF import bug fix for Arrow Tracker. Backend QR extraction working (7/7 tests pass). Frontend Score Keeping screen loads correctly with all components. Code review confirms correct implementation of expo-file-system/legacy import and Android fallback logic.

minor.issue: zbar library was missing system dependency (libzbar0)
minor.resolution: Installed libzbar0 and restarted backend - now working

test_cases.test_extract_qr_with_valid_pdf: PASS - QR extraction from valid PDF with arrow_tracker data works correctly

frontend_tests.import_pdf_qr_button: PASS - Green 'Import from PDF (QR Code)' button visible
frontend_tests.add_archer_modal: PASS - Modal opens with Archer Name, Bow Type selector, Total Score inputs

bug_fix_verification.import_change: Line 21: Correctly uses expo-file-system/legacy instead of expo-file-system
bug_fix_verification.document_picker_config: Line 144: copyToCacheDirectory: true ensures files are accessible
bug_fix_verification.android_fallback: Lines 176-196: Platform-specific fallback copies content:// URIs to cache before reading
bug_fix_verification.error_handling: Lines 205-211: Improved error messages show which files failed

critical_code_review_comments: The expo-file-system/legacy import is the correct fix for Android content:// URI reading issues
Fallback logic for Android properly handles cases where direct read fails
NOTE: Full PDF import flow with DocumentPicker cannot be tested in web browser - requires actual Android/iOS device with PDF file selection
context_for_next_testing_agent: QR code PDF import bug fix verified. Backend /api/extract-qr endpoint working correctly with libzbar0 installed. Frontend code has correct expo-file-system/legacy import and Android fallback logic. The actual file picker flow cannot be tested in web browser but code logic is correct.
limitation_note: The DocumentPicker file selection (expo-document-picker) works only on native devices. Web browser testing cannot fully verify the Android file reading fix - would need actual Android device testing to confirm the content:// URI handling works correctly.
